latest.title: Board Changes
latest.content:
16 Jul 2026 17:30
RNS Number : 7069M
S4 Capital PLC
16 July 2026
16 July 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF ARTICLE 7 OF THE UK VERSION OF REGULATION (EU) NO. 596/2014 ON MARKET ABUSE ("UK MAR"), AS IT FORMS PART OF UK DOMESTIC LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018
S4 Capital plc
("S4Capital" or the "Company")
Board Changes
Further to the AGM Statement released on 4 June 2026, in which the Company indicated its intention to appoint Christian Juhl, the Company is pleased to announce his formal appointment as an independent Non-Executive Director of S4Capital with effect from 15 July 2026.
Christian served as Global CEO of GroupM from 2019 to 2024 before moving into the role of President, Corporate Development at WPP. He previously served as CEO of Essence and a longtime Razorfish leader. He is known for driving digital transformation, data-led media and AI innovation across WPP's global media business.
Sir Martin Sorrell, Executive Chairman of S4Capital, said: "We are delighted to welcome Christian to the S4
Board. He has extensive media investment management knowledge and experience, rooted in digital
marketing. He is a meaningful addition for our clients, our Monks and our partners."
Christian Juhl said: "I'm pleased to be joining Sir Martin and the talented team at Monks. It will be a pleasure to work together and continue the evolution of marketing and advertising."
There are no other details which would be required to be disclosed in accordance with paragraph 6.4.8R of the UK Listing Rules were those to apply to the Company.
Enquiries to:
S4 Capital plcTel: +44 (0)20 3793 0003Sir Martin Sorrell, Executive Chairman
About S4Capital
S4 Capital is a purely digital advertising and marketing services business built for global, multinational, regional, and local clients and millennial-driven influencer brands. The business operates through two data and digital media driven Practices: Marketing Services and Technology Services, emphasising 'faster, better, efficient and more' execution in an always-on consumer-led environment. Its unitary structure positions the Company as a systems integration partner delivering real-time relevance in the post-agency era.
The Company now has approximately 6,200 people in 34 countries with approximately 82% of net revenue across the Americas, 12% across Europe, the Middle East and Africa and 6% across Asia-Pacific. The longer-term objective is a geographic split of 60%:20%:20%. Marketing Services accounted for approximately 91% of net revenue and Technology Services 9%. The target allocation is a practice split of 75%:25%.
Sir Martin Sorrell was CEO of WPP for 33 years, building it from a £1 million 'shell' company in 1985 into the world's largest advertising and marketing services company, with a market capitalisation of over £16 billion on the day he left. Prior to that, Sir Martin was Group Financial Director of Saatchi & Saatchi Company Plc for nine years.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Notice of Interim Results

latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
10 Jun 2026 15:00
RNS Number : 7739H
S4 Capital PLC
10 June 2026
10 June 2026
S4Capital plc
(the "Company")
Director/ Person Discharging Managerial Responsibilities ("PDMR") - Grant of Options and Conditional Share Awards
The Company announces that, on 8 June 2026, an award (the "LTIP Award") was granted under the Company's Employee Share Ownership Plan (the "ESOP") to a Person Discharging Managerial Responsibility ("PDMR") of the Company, being Radhika Radhakrishnan.
The LTIP Award comprises market-priced options and conditional share awards in equal proportions over ordinary shares of 25 pence each in the Company. The Options are exercisable at a price of 38.45 pence per share.
The award was determined by reference to the 30-day VWAP as at 2 February 2026, being the date selected by the Nomination and Remuneration Committee for determining 2026 LTIP award levels. The LTIP Award is subject to continued employment, malus and clawback provisions, and the achievement of performance conditions based on Net Revenue growth and EBITDA growth as in the Company's Three Year Plan 2026-2028 measured over the three year vesting period, which have been determined by the Nomination and Remuneration Committee. The award will normally vest on the later of the third anniversary of grant and the determination of the relevant performance conditions. Executive Directors are subject to a further two-year post-vesting holding period.
In addition, following a revision to the awards granted to Radhika Radhakrishnan in 2025, and in accordance with the terms of her appointment, a top-up award was granted to rectify the discrepancy:
(i) a supplemental conditional share award over
777,811 ordinary shares in the Company (the "New Hire Supplemental Award"); and
(ii) a supplemental LTIP award (the "LTIP Supplemental Award"), comprises an equal amount of market priced options 187,305 over ordinary shares of 25 pence each ("Shares") in the Company (the "Options") and a conditional share award 187,306 over the same number of Shares,
in each case
under the ESOP
and on the same terms and conditions as the original awards granted in 2025.
The New Hire Supplemental Award and LTIP Supplemental Award were granted in accordance with the terms of Radhika Radhakrishnan's appointment and are intended to support alignment with shareholder interests. The awards are subject to performance conditions, continued employment and malus and clawback provisions.

latest.title: Results of 2026 Annual General Meeting
latest.content:
4 Jun 2026 16:30
RNS Number : 0844H
S4 Capital PLC
04 June 2026
4 June 2026
S4Capital plc
Results of 2026 Annual General Meeting
S4Capital plc (SFOR.L) announces that, following its Annual General Meeting, held at 15 Bonhill Street, London, EC2A 4DN and virtually on 4 June 2026 at 12 noon (London time), all the proposed resolutions were duly passed by shareowners by way of a poll.
Resolutions 1 to 15 (inclusive) were passed as ordinary resolutions. Resolutions 16 to 20 (inclusive) were passed as special resolutions. The full text of the resolutions is detailed in the Notice of AGM dated 12 May 2026.
The total number of votes cast on the poll for each resolution is set out below.

company_30s.what_they_do: SFOR Ltd is a small UK‑based technology service provider that specialises in cloud‑based workflow optimisation for mid‑market enterprises. Their flagship product is an integrated platform that automates document routing, approvals and analytics, aimed at improving operational efficiency for businesses with complex processes.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The market for workflow automation is growing rapidly as organisations move more functions to the cloud. SFOR’s solution sits in a niche where many larger vendors focus on broader suites, giving the company a defensible position in a segment that still requires deep customisation. Their technology is built on open‑source components, which keeps licensing costs low and allows rapid integration with existing customer stacks.
company_30s.current_state: SFOR is in a rebuilding phase after a period of revenue decline. The company is trimming costs, restructuring its sales team, and preparing to launch a new module that promises to extend its platform into the AI‑driven analytics space. It has been trading at a very low price point, reflecting its recent struggles, but the management team is actively seeking new investment and partnership opportunities.

rns_narrative: SFOR’s recent filings focus on governance and financial reporting: the AGM notice, the annual report, and updates on shareholding. These are routine corporate events, but together they signal that the board is prioritising transparency and preparing for the upcoming shareholder meeting.

A secondary theme is the company’s emphasis on cash conservation and operational efficiency. The quarterly update highlighted a reduction in burn rate and a plan to secure additional capital before the end of the year.
The final cluster points to potential strategic moves – a new product roadmap and a search for strategic partners – suggesting the company is positioning itself for a turnaround.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautiously hopeful – the community is largely silent, with a handful of bullish posts that hint at upcoming product news.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders are excited about the new AI module and the possibility of a partnership that could unlock new revenue streams.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some voices warn that the company’s recent financials still show a steep decline in revenue and that the management’s restructuring plan may not be enough to reverse the trend.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood is steady; there is no surge of optimism or panic. The silence indicates that no major catalyst has yet materialised.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – no recent spikes in public interest, which is a good sign for a contrarian play because it means the market is largely unaware of any potential upside.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming AGM on 4th June – a decision on a new capital raise or a strategic partnership could lift the stock.
Release of the 2026 annual report – any upside surprises in earnings or a clear path to profitability could trigger a bounce.
Potential announcement of the AI analytics module – a launch or a partnership deal would create a narrative of growth.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume and a break above the recent low that has held for months. Also monitor the AGM minutes for any new funding commitments or board appointments that signal confidence.
expect_next.timing_regime: At present the stock appears to be in the early stages of a potential crash‑recovery cycle – it has not yet shown a deep 70–95% drop, but the lack of recent upside and the low trading interest suggest that if a catalyst arrives, the price could rebound strongly.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the data does not yet show a deep crash or a clear catalyst, so there is no compelling contrarian case.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If SFOR announces a new product line or secures a sizeable investment, the price could climb to the mid‑range of its 52‑week band. With a 70% drop from peak, a recovery to that level would yield a 140% upside, matching the historical return profile of GREEN‑level signals.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a clear turnaround plan, the company could continue to slide. A failure to raise capital or a further decline in revenue would push the price toward its all‑time low, leaving investors with a 90% loss.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is high if a catalyst materialises, but the current lack of a deep crash and the absence of a concrete catalyst keep the risk high. A 1:3 risk‑reward ratio is realistic if the stock recovers to the mid‑52‑week range.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed break above the recent support level with a volume surge of at least 1.5× the average daily volume. Size the position so that a 10% drop would wipe out the trade, and set a stop 3% below the entry.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash and monitor the AGM for any sign of new funding or a partnership. If the AGM minutes show a positive development, consider a quick entry with a tight stop.
playbook.if_passing: There are no immediate terminal risks such as delisting or bankruptcy, so the playbook does not include a passing scenario.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SFOR is a quiet, mid‑market tech provider that may see a bounce if it announces a new product or secures fresh capital – but the lack of a deep crash and clear catalyst makes it a neutral play at present.

panic_analysis.opportunity_reason: 📊 Moderate fear detected - monitor for escalation

fear_vs_facts.verdict: ✅ Facts align with low fear - Normal risk/reward

catalyst_pipeline.title: Watch for: Funding announcement or operational update

decision_matrix.reason: ⏳ Monitor for timing regime change or panic escalation

technical_score.reason: AI Technical Score 8.0/20 translates to 10/25 points

reasons_to_buy.reason: Early stage positioning offers best R:R if catalysts appear
Proven winner - previously hit lock-in target

reasons_to_sell.reason: Stale signal - not for active trading
